passage_title: Should we try to bring extinct species back to life?

sentences.sentence: The passenger pigeon was a legendary species.
sentences.translation: 旅鸽曾是一个传奇物种。

structure.text: The passenger pigeon
was a legendary species

difficult_points: legendary 在这里强调该物种因数量庞大和历史故事而著名，不是单纯表示虚构。
句子使用一般过去时 was，暗示该物种已经不复存在。

sentences.sentence: Flying in vast numbers across North America, with potentially many millions within a single flock, their migration was once one of nature's great spectacles.
sentences.translation: 它们曾以庞大的数量飞越北美，一个鸟群中可能有数百万只；它们的迁徙一度是自然界的壮观奇景之一。

structure.text: Flying in vast numbers across North America
with potentially many millions within a single flock
their migration
was once one of nature's great spectacles

difficult_points: 句首 Flying in vast numbers 是现在分词短语，补充说明旅鸽迁徙时的状态。
with potentially many millions within a single flock 插入说明单个鸟群的规模。
their 指代前一句的 passenger pigeon，而不是泛指所有鸟类。

expressions.meaning_zh: 自然界的壮观景象之一

sentences.sentence: Sadly, the passenger pigeon's existence came to an end on 1 September 1914, when the last living specimen died at Cincinnati Zoo.
sentences.translation: 遗憾的是，旅鸽的存在在 1914 年 9 月 1 日画上了句号，当时最后一只活体标本死于辛辛那提动物园。

structure.text: Sadly
the passenger pigeon's existence
came to an end on 1 September 1914
when the last living specimen died at Cincinnati Zoo

difficult_points: came to an end 是委婉表达，指该物种最终灭绝。
when 引导时间状语从句，说明灭绝日期对应的具体事件。
specimen 在这里指最后一只存活个体，而不是普通的实验样本。

sentences.sentence: Geneticist Ben Novak is lead researcher on an ambitious project which now aims to bring the bird back to life through a process known as 'de-extinction'.
sentences.translation: 遗传学家本·诺瓦克是一个雄心勃勃项目的首席研究员，该项目如今旨在通过一种名为“反灭绝”的过程让这种鸟重获生命。

structure.text: Geneticist Ben Novak
is lead researcher on an ambitious project
which now aims to bring the bird back to life through a process known as 'de-extinction'
known as 'de-extinction'

difficult_points: Geneticist Ben Novak 中 Geneticist 是身份说明，和人名共同构成主语。
which 引导定语从句，修饰 project，而不是 Ben Novak。
known as 'de-extinction' 是过去分词短语，说明 process 的名称。

sentences.sentence: The basic premise involves using cloning technology to turn the DNA of extinct animals into a fertilised embryo, which is carried by the nearest relative still in existence -- in this case, the abundant band-tailed pigeon -- before being born as a living, breathing animal.
sentences.translation: 这一基本设想包括利用克隆技术，把灭绝动物的 DNA 转化为受精胚胎；该胚胎由仍然存在的最近亲物种携带——在这里就是数量丰富的斑尾鸽——最后作为一个活生生的动物出生。

structure.text: The basic premise
involves using cloning technology
to turn the DNA of extinct animals into a fertilised embryo
which is carried by the nearest relative still in existence -- in this case, the abundant band-tailed pigeon --
before being born as a living, breathing animal

difficult_points: using cloning technology 是 involves 的宾语部分，说明这一设想包含的操作。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 embryo，说明胚胎由谁携带。
双连字符中间的 in this case 插入具体例子，不改变主句结构。
before being born 使用动名词被动形式，表示出生之前的阶段。

sentences.sentence: Passenger pigeons are one of the pioneering species in this field, but they are far from the only ones on which this cutting-edge technology is being trialled.
sentences.translation: 旅鸽是这一领域的先行实验物种之一，但它们远不是唯一正在接受这项前沿技术试验的物种。

structure.text: Passenger pigeons
are one of the pioneering species in this field
but they are far from the only ones
on which this cutting-edge technology is being trialled

difficult_points: but 连接两个并列分句，第二个分句强调旅鸽并非唯一对象。
far from the only ones 表示“远非唯一”，语气比 not the only ones 更强。
on which 引导定语从句，修饰 ones，表示技术在这些物种身上被试验。

sentences.sentence: In Australia, the thylacine, more commonly known as the Tasmanian tiger, is another extinct creature which genetic scientists are striving to bring back to life.
sentences.translation: 在澳大利亚，袋狼——更常被称为塔斯马尼亚虎——是另一种遗传科学家正努力复活的灭绝生物。

structure.text: In Australia
the thylacine
more commonly known as the Tasmanian tiger
is another extinct creature
which genetic scientists are striving to bring back to life

difficult_points: more commonly known as the Tasmanian tiger 是插入的过去分词短语，说明 thylacine 的通称。
which 引导定语从句，修饰 another extinct creature。
are striving to 表示正在努力做某事，强调复活项目仍在进行。

sentences.sentence: 'There is no carnivore now in Tasmania that fills the niche which thylacines once occupied,' explains Michael Archer of the University of New South Wales.
sentences.translation: “如今塔斯马尼亚没有食肉动物填补袋狼曾经占据的生态位，”新南威尔士大学的迈克尔·阿彻解释说。

structure.text: 'There is no carnivore now in Tasmania
that fills the niche
which thylacines once occupied
explains Michael Archer of the University of New South Wales

difficult_points: There is no carnivore 是存在句结构，说明某类动物不存在。
that fills the niche 修饰 carnivore，说明缺失的是能承担该生态功能的食肉动物。
which thylacines once occupied 进一步修饰 niche，说明这个生态位原本属于袋狼。

sentences.sentence: He points out that in the decades since the thylacine went extinct, there has been a spread in a 'dangerously debilitating' facial tumour syndrome which threatens the existence of the Tasmanian devils, the island's other notorious resident.
sentences.translation: 他指出，在袋狼灭绝后的几十年里，一种“危险且使动物虚弱”的面部肿瘤综合征已经扩散，威胁到塔斯马尼亚恶魔这一岛上另一种著名动物的存在。

structure.text: He
points out

that in the decades since the thylacine went extinct, there has been a spread in a 'dangerously debilitating' facial tumour syndrome which threatens the existence of the Tasmanian devils, the island's other notorious resident
since the thylacine went extinct
which threatens the existence of the Tasmanian devils, the island's other notorious resident

difficult_points: that 引导 points out 的宾语从句，后面包含完整的 there be 结构。
since the thylacine went extinct 修饰时间范围 in the decades。
which threatens... 修饰 facial tumour syndrome，说明扩散问题的后果。
the island's other notorious resident 是 Tasmanian devils 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: Thylacines would have prevented this spread because they would have killed significant numbers of Tasmanian devils.
sentences.translation: 袋狼本来会阻止这种扩散，因为它们会捕杀大量塔斯马尼亚恶魔。

structure.text: Thylacines
would have prevented this spread
because they would have killed significant numbers of Tasmanian devils

difficult_points: would have prevented 使用虚拟语气，表示如果袋狼还存在，本可阻止扩散。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释袋狼能阻止疾病扩散的机制。
significant numbers of 表示数量相当可观，不是指“重要的数字”。

sentences.sentence: 'If that contagious cancer had popped up previously, it would have burned out in whatever region it started.
sentences.translation: “如果那种传染性癌症以前出现过，它本会在其最初出现的任何地区自行消亡。

structure.text: 'If that contagious cancer had popped up previously
it
would have burned out
in whatever region it started

difficult_points: had popped up 与 would have burned out 构成对过去情况的虚拟条件句。
pop up 在这里指疾病突然出现。
whatever region it started 表示无论疾病最初在哪个区域出现。

sentences.sentence: The return of thylacines to Tasmania could help to ensure that devils are never again subjected to risks of this kind.'
sentences.translation: 袋狼重返塔斯马尼亚可能有助于确保塔斯马尼亚恶魔不再遭受这类风险。”

structure.text: The return of thylacines to Tasmania
could help
to ensure that devils are never again subjected to risks of this kind
that devils are never again subjected to risks of this kind

difficult_points: The return of thylacines to Tasmania 是名词短语主语，核心词是 return。
to ensure 后接 that 从句，说明“确保”的具体内容。
are subjected to 是被动结构，表示 devils 承受风险。

sentences.sentence: If extinct species can be brought back to life, can humanity begin to correct the damage it has caused to the natural world over the past few millennia?
sentences.translation: 如果灭绝物种能够被复活，人类是否就能开始纠正过去几千年里自己对自然界造成的破坏？

structure.text: If extinct species can be brought back to life
can humanity begin
to correct the damage
it has caused to the natural world over the past few millennia

difficult_points: If 从句提出假设，主句用疑问形式讨论这一技术的伦理意义。
it has caused 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 the damage。
over the past few millennia 表示时间跨度是过去几千年。

sentences.sentence: 'The idea of de-extinction is that we can reverse this process, bringing species that no longer exist back to life,' says Beth Shapiro of University of California Santa Cruz's Genomics Institute.
sentences.translation: “反灭绝的理念是，我们可以逆转这一过程，把已经不复存在的物种带回生命之中，”加州大学圣克鲁兹分校基因组学研究所的贝丝·夏皮罗说。

structure.text: 'The idea of de-extinction
is that we can reverse this process
bringing species that no longer exist back to life
that no longer exist
says Beth Shapiro of University of California Santa Cruz's Genomics Institute

difficult_points: is that... 是表语从句结构，说明 idea 的具体内容。
bringing species... 是现在分词短语，补充说明 reverse this process 的结果。
that no longer exist 修饰 species，强调这些物种已经不存在。

sentences.sentence: 'I don't think that we can do this.
sentences.translation: “我不认为我们能做到这一点。

structure.text: 'I
don't think
that we can do this

difficult_points: don't think that... 是否定前移，实际否定的是从句中“我们能做到”。
this 指代前文所说的把灭绝物种完整复活。
开头的单引号表示这句话与下一句同属一段直接引语。

sentences.sentence: There is no way to bring back something that is 100 per cent identical to a species that went extinct a long time ago.'
sentences.translation: 不可能让某个东西回归，并且与很久以前灭绝的某个物种百分之百完全相同。”

structure.text: There
is no way to bring back something
that is 100 per cent identical to a species
that went extinct a long time ago

difficult_points: There is no way to... 表示“没有办法做……”，语气很绝对。
第一个 that 修饰 something，说明复活物不可能与原物种完全相同。
第二个 that 修饰 a species，说明该物种很久以前已经灭绝。

sentences.sentence: A more practical approach for long-extinct species is to take the DNA of existing species as a template, ready for the insertion of strands of extinct animal DNA to create something new; a hybrid, based on the living species, but which looks and/or acts like the animal which died out.
sentences.translation: 对于早已灭绝的物种，更实际的方法是以现存物种的 DNA 为模板，准备插入灭绝动物的 DNA 片段，从而创造出某种新的东西；也就是一种以现存物种为基础、但外观和/或行为像已经灭绝动物的杂交体。

structure.text: A more practical approach for long-extinct species
is to take the DNA of existing species as a template
ready for the insertion of strands of extinct animal DNA
to create something new
a hybrid, based on the living species
but which looks and/or acts like the animal which died out

difficult_points: 主语是 A more practical approach，后面的 for long-extinct species 限定适用对象。
分号后的 a hybrid 是对 something new 的进一步解释。
based on the living species 是过去分词短语，说明 hybrid 的基础。
which died out 修饰 the animal，指已经灭绝的动物。

sentences.sentence: This complicated process and questionable outcome begs the question: what is the actual point of this technology?
sentences.translation: 这一复杂过程和可疑结果引出了一个问题：这项技术的真正意义究竟是什么？

structure.text: This complicated process and questionable outcome
begs the question
what is the actual point of this technology

difficult_points: This complicated process and questionable outcome 指代上一段提出的基因混合式复活方案。
begs the question 在这里表示“引出问题”，不是逻辑学中的“循环论证”。
冒号后的疑问句解释前面 the question 的具体内容。

expressions.meaning_zh: 可疑的结果；存疑的结果

sentences.sentence: 'For us, the goal has always been replacing the extinct species with a suitable replacement,' explains Novak.
sentences.translation: “对我们来说，目标一直是用合适的替代物来替换已灭绝物种，”诺瓦克解释说。

structure.text: 'For us
the goal
has always been replacing the extinct species with a suitable replacement
explains Novak

difficult_points: For us 限定说话者团队的目标，不是所有科学家的共同目标。
has always been replacing 使用现在完成进行意义，强调目标从过去到现在一直如此。
replacement 重复出现，突出复活技术并非追求完全复制原物种。

sentences.sentence: 'When it comes to breeding, band-tailed pigeons scatter and make maybe one or two nests per hectare, whereas passenger pigeons were very social and would make 10,000 or more nests in one hectare.'
sentences.translation: “说到繁殖，斑尾鸽会分散开来，每公顷也许只筑一两个巢；而旅鸽非常群居，每公顷会筑一万个或更多的巢。”

structure.text: 'When it comes to breeding
band-tailed pigeons
scatter and make maybe one or two nests per hectare
whereas passenger pigeons were very social and would make 10,000 or more nests in one hectare

difficult_points: When it comes to breeding 表示“谈到繁殖方面”，限定比较的主题。
scatter and make 是并列谓语，共同说明斑尾鸽的繁殖习性。
whereas 引导对比从句，突出斑尾鸽与旅鸽筑巢密度的巨大差异。

sentences.sentence: Since the disappearance of this key species, ecosystems in the eastern US have suffered, as the lack of disturbance caused by thousands of passenger pigeons wrecking trees and branches means there has been minimal need for regrowth.
sentences.translation: 自这一关键物种消失以来，美国东部的生态系统已经受损，因为成千上万只旅鸽破坏树木和树枝所造成的扰动不复存在，这意味着森林几乎没有再生的需求。

structure.text: Since the disappearance of this key species
ecosystems in the eastern US
have suffered
as the lack of disturbance caused by thousands of passenger pigeons wrecking trees and branches means there has been minimal need for regrowth
caused by thousands of passenger pigeons wrecking trees and branches

difficult_points: Since 在这里是介词，后接名词短语 the disappearance，而不是从句。
as 引导原因状语从句，解释生态系统受损的原因。
caused by... 是过去分词短语，修饰 disturbance。
there has been minimal need for regrowth 是 means 的宾语从句，省略了 that。

sentences.sentence: This has left forests stagnant and therefore unwelcoming to the plants and animals which evolved to help regenerate the forest after a disturbance.
sentences.translation: 这使森林陷入停滞，因此不再适合那些进化出在扰动后帮助森林再生的动植物生存。

structure.text: This
has left forests stagnant and therefore unwelcoming
to the plants and animals
which evolved to help regenerate the forest after a disturbance

difficult_points: This 指代前一句所说的再生需求减少这一生态后果。
has left forests stagnant 使用 leave + 宾语 + 补足语结构，表示造成某种状态。
which 引导定语从句，修饰 the plants and animals。

sentences.sentence: According to Novak, a hybridised band-tailed pigeon, with the added nesting habits of a passenger pigeon, could, in theory, re-establish that forest disturbance, thereby creating a habitat necessary for a great many other native species to thrive.
sentences.translation: 据诺瓦克所说，一种带有旅鸽筑巢习性的杂交斑尾鸽理论上能够重新建立那种森林扰动，从而创造出许多其他本土物种繁荣所必需的栖息地。

structure.text: According to Novak
a hybridised band-tailed pigeon
with the added nesting habits of a passenger pigeon
could, in theory, re-establish that forest disturbance
thereby creating a habitat necessary for a great many other native species to thrive

difficult_points: According to Novak 标明观点来源，不属于句子主干。
with the added nesting habits... 插入说明这种杂交鸽新增的特征。
in theory 表示该效果仍是理论上的可能性。
thereby creating... 表示前一动作带来的结果。

sentences.sentence: Another popular candidate for this technology is the woolly mammoth.
sentences.translation: 这项技术的另一个热门候选对象是长毛猛犸象。

structure.text: Another popular candidate for this technology
is the woolly mammoth

difficult_points: candidate 在这里指适合被该技术尝试复活的候选物种。
for this technology 修饰 candidate，说明候选关系对应的是反灭绝技术。

sentences.sentence: George Church, professor at Harvard Medical School and leader of the Woolly Mammoth Revival Project, has been focusing on cold resistance, the main way in which the extinct woolly mammoth and its nearest living relative, the Asian elephant, differ.
sentences.translation: 乔治·丘奇是哈佛医学院教授，也是长毛猛犸象复兴项目的负责人；他一直专注于抗寒性，这是已经灭绝的长毛猛犸象与其最近的现存亲属亚洲象之间的主要差异。

structure.text: George Church
professor at Harvard Medical School and leader of the Woolly Mammoth Revival Project
has been focusing on cold resistance
the main way in which the extinct woolly mammoth and its nearest living relative, the Asian elephant, differ
in which the extinct woolly mammoth and its nearest living relative, the Asian elephant, differ

difficult_points: professor... and leader... 是 George Church 的同位语，补充身份。
has been focusing on 使用现在完成进行时，表示研究重点持续到现在。
the main way... 是 cold resistance 的同位说明，解释为什么关注抗寒性。
the Asian elephant 是 its nearest living relative 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: By pinpointing which genetic traits made it possible for mammoths to survive the icy climate of the tundra, the project's goal is to return mammoths, or a mammoth-like species, to the area.
sentences.translation: 通过精确找出哪些遗传特征使猛犸象能够在苔原的冰冷气候中生存，该项目的目标是让猛犸象或类似猛犸象的物种重返该地区。

structure.text: By pinpointing which genetic traits made it possible for mammoths to survive the icy climate of the tundra
which genetic traits made it possible for mammoths to survive the icy climate of the tundra
the project's goal
is to return mammoths, or a mammoth-like species, to the area

difficult_points: By pinpointing... 表示实现项目目标所依赖的方法。
which genetic traits... 是 pinpointing 的宾语从句。
made it possible for mammoths to survive 使用 make + it + adjective + for...to... 结构。
or a mammoth-like species 是插入替代对象，说明目标不一定是真正的猛犸象。

sentences.sentence: 'My highest priority would be preserving the endangered Asian elephant,' says Church, 'expanding their range to the huge ecosystem of the tundra.
sentences.translation: “我的最高优先事项会是保护濒危的亚洲象，”丘奇说，“把它们的活动范围扩展到巨大的苔原生态系统中。

structure.text: 'My highest priority
would be preserving the endangered Asian elephant
says Church
'expanding their range to the huge ecosystem of the tundra

difficult_points: would be preserving 表示设想中的优先目标，而不是已经完成的行动。
their 指代 endangered Asian elephant，语义上指亚洲象这一物种群体。
expanding their range 是现在分词短语，说明保护亚洲象的具体方式之一。

sentences.sentence: Necessary adaptations would include smaller ears, thicker hair, and extra insulating fat, all for the purpose of reducing heat loss in the tundra, and all traits found in the now extinct woolly mammoth.'
sentences.translation: 必要的适应性改变会包括更小的耳朵、更厚的毛发和额外的隔热脂肪，全都是为了在苔原中减少热量流失，也全都是如今已经灭绝的长毛猛犸象所具有的特征。”

structure.text: Necessary adaptations
would include smaller ears, thicker hair, and extra insulating fat
all for the purpose of reducing heat loss in the tundra
and all traits found in the now extinct woolly mammoth

difficult_points: Necessary adaptations 指为了适应苔原环境需要加入或强化的身体特征。
all for the purpose of... 回指前面列出的 ears, hair 和 fat。
found in the now extinct woolly mammoth 是过去分词短语，修饰 traits。

sentences.sentence: This repopulation of the tundra and boreal forests of Eurasia and North America with large mammals could also be a useful factor in reducing carbon emissions -- elephants punch holes through snow and knock down trees, which encourages grass growth.
sentences.translation: 让大型哺乳动物重新遍布欧亚大陆和北美的苔原与北方针叶林，也可能成为减少碳排放的一个有用因素——大象会在积雪中踩出洞并撞倒树木，这会促进草地生长。

structure.text: This repopulation of the tundra and boreal forests of Eurasia and North America with large mammals
could also be a useful factor in reducing carbon emissions
elephants punch holes through snow and knock down trees
which encourages grass growth

difficult_points: This repopulation...with large mammals 是长主语，核心词是 repopulation。
双连字符后的内容解释为什么大型哺乳动物能帮助减少碳排放。
which encourages grass growth 指代前面大象踩雪和推倒树木这一整体行为。

sentences.sentence: This grass growth would reduce temperatures, and mitigate emissions from melting permafrost.
sentences.translation: 这种草地生长会降低温度，并缓解永久冻土融化所产生的排放。

structure.text: This grass growth
would reduce temperatures
and mitigate emissions from melting permafrost

difficult_points: This grass growth 指代上一句提到的大象活动所促进的草地扩张。
reduce 和 mitigate 是并列谓语动作，第二个谓语省略了 would。
from melting permafrost 修饰 emissions，说明排放来源。

sentences.sentence: While the prospect of bringing extinct animals back to life might capture imaginations, it is, of course, far easier to try to save an existing species which is merely threatened with extinction.
sentences.translation: 虽然让灭绝动物复活的前景或许会激发人们的想象，但当然，试图拯救一个只是面临灭绝威胁的现存物种要容易得多。

structure.text: While the prospect of bringing extinct animals back to life might capture imaginations
it
is, of course, far easier
to try to save an existing species
which is merely threatened with extinction

difficult_points: While 引导让步状语从句，承认反灭绝有吸引力，再转向更现实的保护工作。
it 是形式主语，真正主语是不定式 to try to save...。
which is merely threatened with extinction 修饰 an existing species，说明该物种尚未灭绝。

sentences.sentence: 'Many of the technologies that people have in mind when they think about de-extinction can be used as a form of "genetic rescue",' explains Shapiro.
sentences.translation: “人们想到反灭绝时脑海中的许多技术，都可以作为一种‘基因救援’形式来使用，”夏皮罗解释说。

structure.text: 'Many of the technologies
that people have in mind
when they think about de-extinction
can be used as a form of "genetic rescue"
explains Shapiro

difficult_points: that people have in mind 修饰 technologies，表示人们想到的技术。
when they think about de-extinction 嵌在定语从句中，说明想到这些技术的语境。
can be used 是被动结构，强调技术的用途而不是使用者。

sentences.sentence: She prefers to focus the debate on how this emerging technology could be used to fully understand why various species went extinct in the first place, and therefore how we could use it to make genetic modifications which could prevent mass extinctions in the future.
sentences.translation: 她更愿意把讨论集中在这样的问题上：这项新兴技术如何能够被用来充分理解各种物种最初为何灭绝，以及因此我们如何能用它进行基因改造，从而防止未来的大规模灭绝。

structure.text: She
prefers to focus the debate
on how this emerging technology could be used to fully understand why various species went extinct in the first place
and therefore how we could use it to make genetic modifications
which could prevent mass extinctions in the future

difficult_points: prefers to focus the debate on... 表示她希望改变讨论重点。
how this emerging technology could be used... 和 how we could use it... 是并列的宾语内容。
why various species went extinct in the first place 嵌在 understand 后，说明要理解的原因。
which could prevent... 修饰 genetic modifications，说明基因改造的潜在作用。

sentences.sentence: 'I would also say there's an incredible moral hazard to not do anything at all,' she continues.
sentences.translation: “我还想说，完全不采取任何行动也存在巨大的道德风险，”她继续说道。

structure.text: 'I
would also say
there's an incredible moral hazard to not do anything at all
she continues

difficult_points: would also say 表示进一步补充观点，语气较为委婉。
there's... 是存在句，指出不行动本身也有风险。
to not do anything at all 是不定式短语，说明 moral hazard 的来源。

sentences.sentence: 'We know that what we are doing today is not enough, and we have to be willing to take some calculated and measured risks.'
sentences.translation: “我们知道，我们今天所做的还不够，而且我们必须愿意承担一些经过计算和衡量的风险。”

structure.text: 'We
know
that what we are doing today is not enough
what we are doing today
and we have to be willing to take some calculated and measured risks

difficult_points: that 引导 know 的宾语从句，说明已经认识到的事实。
what we are doing today 是名词性从句，在 that 从句中作主语。
have to be willing to 表示不仅必须采取行动，还必须愿意承担风险。
calculated and measured 修饰 risks，强调风险经过评估而非鲁莽冒险。

expressions.meaning_zh: 经过计算和衡量的风险
